t: Amy counts by 5s starting at 280. She says 280, 285, 290, 300. What mistake did she make?
o: She started at the wrong number
She counted by 10s at the end
e: Counting by 5s from 290 should give 295, then 300. Amy jumped from 290 to 300, skipping 295.

t: ___ is 100 more than 867 and 10 less than 977. What is ___?
e: 100 more than 867: 867 + 100 = 967. Check: 10 less than 977: 977 - 10 = 967. Both clues give 967!

t: Kai counts by 10s from 8. Which number will he NOT land on?
e: From 8, counting by 10s: 18, 28, 38, 48, 58, 68, 78, 88, 98... The ones digit always stays 8. He will never land on 50.

t: Dani starts at 1,200 and counts backward by 100s. Which number will she say third?
e: First: 1,100. Second: 1,000. Third: 900. Count backward by 100s three times from 1,200.

t: Zoe says: '10 less than 305 is 295.' Her teacher says: '10 less than 305 is 304.' Who is right?
e: 305 - 10 = 295. Zoe is correct! The teacher subtracted 1 instead of 10. When you take away 10 from 305, the tens go from 0 to 9 and hundreds drop: 295.

t: Sam starts at 250 and adds 100 four times. Then he subtracts 10 three times. Where does he end up?
e: Start: 250. Add 100 four times: 350, 450, 550, 650. Subtract 10 three times: 640, 630, 620. End: 620.

t: Eva counts by 5s from 0. Max counts by 2s from 0. What is the first number greater than 10 that BOTH will say?
Mia writes: 10 more than 498 is 508. Is she correct?
e: 498 + 10 = 508. Yes! The 9 tens become 10 tens, which is 1 hundred. So hundreds go from 4 to 5, tens go to 0. Correct!

t: Leo counts by 100s: 150, 250, 350, ___, 550. What is the missing number?
e: The pattern adds 100 each time. 350 + 100 = 450. Check: 450 + 100 = 550. The missing number is 450.

t: Nora says that 10 more than 990 is 1,000, and 10 more than 1,000 is 1,100. Find her error.
e: 990 + 10 = 1,000 is correct. But 1,000 + 10 = 1,010, not 1,100. Nora added 100 instead of 10 the second time.

t: Fill in BOTH blanks: 10 more than ___ is 600. 100 less than ___ is 600.
e: 10 more than ? = 600, so ? = 590. 100 less than ? = 600, so ? = 700. Answers: 590 and 700.

t: Tyler counts by 2s from 1. Will he ever say 48?
o: No, because he starts on an odd number
e: Starting at 1 and counting by 2s: 1, 3, 5, 7, 9... He only lands on odd numbers. 48 is even, so he will never say it.

t: Ava has 780. She adds 10 three times and then adds 100 once. What does she have now?
Pedro says 100 less than 1,005 is 905. Ben says it is 995. Who is right?
e: 1,005 - 100 = 905. Pedro is right. Ben subtracted 10 instead of 100.

t: Look at the pattern: 1,200, 1,100, 1,000, 900, ___. What rule does this follow, and what comes next?
e: Each number is 100 less than the one before it. 900 - 100 = 800. The rule is subtract 100.

t: Rosa starts at 50. She counts by 10s to 100, then counts by 100s from there. Which number will she NOT say?
e: By 10s from 50: 60, 70, 80, 90, 100. Then by 100s from 100: 200, 300, 400... She says 70, 100, and 200 but she jumps from 100 to 200, so she never says 150.

t: What number is 10 more than 10 more than 10 more than 770?
e: Add 10 three times: 770 + 10 = 780, 780 + 10 = 790, 790 + 10 = 800. That is the same as adding 30.

t: Jada says skip counting by 5s will land on every number that ends in 0. Is she right?
o: No, it also lands on numbers ending in 5
No, it only lands on even numbers
No, it skips some numbers ending in 0
e: Counting by 5s from 0: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30... It hits every number ending in 0 AND every number ending in 5. Jada is partially right but forgot about the 5s.

t: Omar starts at 340. He subtracts 10 five times. Then adds 100 twice. Where is he?
Which strategy finds 10 less than 800 fastest?
o: Change the tens digit from 0 to 9 and hundreds from 8 to 7
All give the same answer but B is fastest
e: All three methods give 790. But changing digits directly (B) is fastest: tens 0 becomes 9, hundreds 8 becomes 7 = 790. Strategy B uses place value knowledge.
